title: Fix a worker that stores a Decimal in Oban args
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/decimal_args_worker.ex` passes a `%Decimal{}` through Oban args and pattern-matches on `%Decimal{}` inside perform/1. Decimal has no native JSON representation — Jason encodes it but does not round-trip back to the struct. Rewrite the module so the enqueue helper calls `Decimal.to_string/1` before insert, the args use string keys (`"account_id"`, `"amount"`, `"currency"`), and perform/1 calls `Decimal.new/1` on the amount string before passing it to `Ledger.credit/2`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/ledger_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Specific to Decimal — requires knowing the type is unsafe for direct JSON and reaching for `Decimal.to_string/1`. Sonnet often picks `Jason.encode!/1` or assumes the struct survives.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'Because args are always encoded as JSON, you must also ensure that all values are serializable, otherwise you'll have encoding errors when inserting jobs.'
